mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show positive mean during rate hiking periods with statistical significance (p < 0.05), or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE increases by over 0.3 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their 6-month moving averages
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in at least 70% of observed cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of low inflation reflects healthy supply-side capacity utilization that boosts near-term equity momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns when CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 4.0%, or signal loses predictive power in low-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between rising inflation and stable federal funds rates forces markets to price in imminent monetary tightening, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as discount rates adjust upward.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change exceeds 0.4% while FEDFUNDS remains unchanged
failure_condition: The inflation-rate divergence signal produces positive 20-day returns in over 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect vanishes in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and consumption capacity, providing a fundamental floor for corporate earnings that generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP exceeding 0.5% triggers immediate risk-off positioning as recession fears materialize, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of quarters where GDPC1 contracts by over 0.5%, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates that higher borrowing costs are successfully cooling economic activity, which reduces near-term earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by at least 0.25% in the prior quarter
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during tightening periods yields positive 20-day returns in over 65% of cases, or the regime-specific predictive power disappears after controlling for market volatility.
